title: Dreamy Creamy German Potato Gratin
description: A solo dinner German dish featuring mint and steam, served in a mason jar, with a cultured and creamy texture. This dish is perfect for a cozy night in.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large saucepan, combine the sliced potatoes and enough cold water to cover them, then bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 5 minutes, or until the potatoes are slightly tender.
Drain the potatoes and set them aside.
In a large skillet, melt the butter over medium heat, then add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked potatoes, onion mixture, heavy cream, grated cheese, chopped mint, salt, and black pepper, and mix well.
Transfer the potato mixture to a mason jar and cover with a lid.
Place the mason jar in a large baking dish and add hot water to come halfway up the side of the jar.
Bake for 25 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender and the top is golden brown.
